classObjective: Interpret slope and rate of change in real-world contexts including grade, elevation, and positioning.
workedExamples: 4-2, Example 10 — Slope in Real-World Contexts (Grade, Elevation, Positioning)
warmUp: A road rises 30 feet over a horizontal distance of 150 feet — what's its grade?
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: ADA wheelchair ramp standard (1:12 ratio); road grade interpretation; vertical vs. horizontal change; positioning context
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice interpreting slope in real-world contexts including grade, elevation, and positioning
independentPractice: Practice problems interpreting slope/rate of change in real-world contexts
assessment: Exit ticket: interpret the meaning of a 6% grade on a highway
capReflection: Why is understanding slope important in real-world construction and accessibility contexts?
